text:
There once was a young cub bear who loved exploring. One beautiful forest morning, after waking up from a tasty berry sleep, he bounded through the trees.

As he sniffled through the pine greens, he found a cozy campsite made ofsticks and branches. Purring with excitement, he stretched out beneath a branch sky.

Suddenly, a deep growl shook the air.

Something big lurked. Curiosity drove him out onto a wide clearing where big owl eyes peered down. And there was Old Reliable––a dog––who hadn't aged a day.

"Barking buddies," Old Reliable growled fondly.

Together they explored the woods, playing fetch under the moonlight. That night a gentle snow fell, wrapping them all snug. They slept soundly––Old Reliable curled like a giant pillow.

When morning came, Old Reliable took the lead again.

They traveled northward through quiet woodland paths. The forest sang songs quietly in frostberries.

Their adventure ended when twilight touched all skies.
